channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lebanon's new president strikes a nationalistic tone amid regional shifts, further weakening of Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese parliament finally voted on a new president on Thursday following two years of deadlock. Joseph Aoun the head of the country's armed forces won a majority of the votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moldovans grapple with outages as energy crisis grips pro-Russia region
item.description: COPANCA, Moldova (AP) — Every time planned electrical outages are imposed on his village in southern Moldova, 73-year-old retiree Vasili Donici passes the time solving crosswords and puzzles in a room he illuminates with a small gas lamp.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and AUREL OBREJA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nicolás Maduro is set to be Venezuela's president until 2031
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — President Nicolás Maduro will extend his increasingly repressive rule over Venezuela until 2031 when he is sworn in on Friday, despite credible evidence that his opponent won the latest election and following protests against his plan to serve a third six-year term.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tanker hit by Houthi rebels that threatened a Red Sea oil spill has been salvaged
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An oil tanker that burned for weeks in the Red Sea after being attacked by Yemen's Houthi rebels and threatening a massive oil spill has been salvaged, a security firm said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Body recovered in Gaza confirmed to be 23-year-old hostage Hamzah AlZayadni
item.description: Israel’s army has confirmed that one of the bodies recovered from Gaza earlier this week was that of 23-year-old hostage Hamzah AlZayadni.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: Estonian naval ships are taking part in stepped-up patrols in the Baltic Sea by NATO countries after undersea power and communications cables have been damaged in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows And Hendrik Osula, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An albatross couple shares egg duty in this captivating low-drama reality show
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — It’s a reality show about a loving couple waiting to welcome their new arrival, watched by thousands of ardent fans.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lebanon's parliament elects army commander Joseph Aoun as president, ending a 2-year deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s parliament voted Thursday to elect army commander Joseph Aoun as head of state, filling a more than two-year-long presidential vacuum.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Panama marks Martyrs' Day as Trump threatens to retake control of Panama Canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — A Panamanian national holiday remembering the killing of 21 protesters by police and U.S. troops in 1964 is taking on new resonance this year as U.S. President-elect Donald Trump threatens to take back control of the Panama Canal. On Jan. 9, 1964, students protested in the then-U.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris, Emhoff cancel trip to Singapore, Middle East, Germany amid California fires
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff announced their decision to cancel their upcoming international trips on Thursday in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Poland adopts resolution protecting Netanyahu from arrest if he attends Auschwitz liberation commemoration
item.description: Poland passed a resolution to ensure the safe participation of Israeli representatives who attend 80th anniversary commemorations of the liberation of Auschwitz-Birkenau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, Gaza's Health Ministry said Thursday, with no end in sight to the 15-month conflict. The ministry said a total of 46,006 Palestinians have been killed and 109,378 wounded.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy’s foreign minister heads to Syria to encourage post-Assad transition
item.description: Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani is heading to Syria to encourage the transition in the country following the ouster of President Bashar Assad by Islamist insurgents.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Outgoing ICE director: Biden should have tightened border security sooner
item.description: The outgoing head of the Immigration and Customs Enforcement Agency (ICE) said in an interview Wednesday that President Biden should have tightened border security sooner.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: NOAA says La Nina ocean cooling has finally arrived, but it's weak and may cause fewer problems
item.description: A long-awaited La Nina has finally appeared, but the periodic cooling of Pacific Ocean waters is weak and unlikely to cause as many weather problems as usual, meteorologists said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel sends report to UN on 'brutal' treatment used by terrorists against hostages in Gaza
item.description: In a new report sent to the U.N. by Israel's health ministry, freed hostages reveal systematic abuse by Hamas, as 99 remain in captivity enduring brutal physical and psychological torment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italy's Meloni hopes to attend Trump inauguration as she downplays his Greenland and Panama comments
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday she didn’t believe President-elect Donald Trump actually intends to use military force to seize control of Greenland or the Panama Canal, saying she read his comments more as a warning to China and other global players to keep their hands off
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's Ishiba visiting Malaysia and Indonesia to strengthen defense and economic ties
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba is visiting Malaysia and Indonesia as part of his effort to further strengthen defense and economic ties with Southeast Asia as threats from China rise in the region.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK lawmakers vote against inquiry into 'rape gang scandal' as Musk keeps up pressure
item.description: British lawmakers voted against an amendment to hold a national inquiry into the U.K. grooming scandal, amid international pressure from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: US strikes underground Houthi weapons depots used to hit American ships
item.description: U.S. hit weapon storage facilities used by the Iran-backed Houthi terrorist organization to target U.S. military and merchant ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reinforces 'all hell will break out' if hostages not returned by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump once again threatened "all hell will break out" if the hostages in Gaza are not freed by the time he enters office, though he refused to detail what actions he will take.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's nuclear program is nearing 'the point of no return,' France's Macron says
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is warning that Iran's nuclear program is approaching the "point of no return," calling it a top "security challenge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kim Jong Un’s big guns spotted on Russian front lines: report
item.description: A North Korean howitzer has been reported on Russia's front lines following November sightings of a shipment of two M1989 Koksan self-propelled big guns as Pyongyang entered the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation
